name: Jon Graham Burge
role_title: Commander, Chicago Police Department (Area 2 & Area 3)
charges: Perjury, obstruction of justice (lied about torturing 100+ Black suspects 1972–1991 during civil lawsuit deposition)
outcome: 54 months in federal prison (died in federal custody September 19, 2018)

source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Tanaka sentencing

name: Victor Hill
role_title: Sheriff, Clayton County Sheriff's Office, Georgia
charges: Six counts of deprivation of civil rights under color of law (ordered pretrial detainees strapped into restraint chairs without justification as punishment)

name: Rafael Pérez

name: Nino Durden (Gino Floyd Durden)

name: Arthur Kaufman

name: Anthony Villavaso

name: John Joseph Connolly Jr.
role_title: Special Agent, FBI Boston Field Office
charges: Racketeering, obstruction of justice, bribery (federal, 2002); second-degree murder (Florida state, 2008) — leaked information to Boston mob boss Whitey Bulger and the Winter Hill Gang
jurisdiction: U.S. District Court, D. Massachusetts; Miami-Dade Circuit Court (state)
source1_description: FBI history on Connolly corruption case
source2_description: Boston Globe coverage of Connolly convictions

name: Robert Philip Hanssen
role_title: Supervisory Special Agent, FBI — counterintelligence
charges: 13 counts espionage, 1 attempted espionage, 1 conspiracy to commit espionage (sold classified intelligence to Soviet Union and Russia for 20+ years; died in federal prison June 5, 2023)
outcome: Life without parole (died in custody June 5, 2023)
source1_description: FBI official history on Hanssen espionage case
source2_description: DOJ Press Release on Hanssen sentencing

name: Richard William Miller
role_title: Special Agent, FBI Los Angeles Field Office
charges: Espionage (provided classified FBI information to Soviet KGB; first FBI agent ever convicted of espionage)
outcome: 20 years (third trial conviction; released early 1994; died 2013)
source1_description: FBI history on Miller espionage case
source2_description: Los Angeles Times coverage of Miller case

name: Charles F. McGonigal
role_title: Special Agent in Charge, Counterintelligence, FBI New York
charges: Concealing material facts (Case 1, D.C.); conspiracy to violate IEEPA sanctions on Russia, money laundering — worked for sanctioned Russian oligarch Oleg Deripaska (Case 2, SDNY)
jurisdiction: U.S. District Courts, D.C. and S.D.N.Y.
outcome: 6.5 years total: 50 months SDNY + 28 months D.C., consecutive
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on McGonigal SDNY sentencing
source2_description: Washington Post coverage of McGonigal case

name: José Irizarry
role_title: Special Agent, Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA), Miami
charges: 19 counts: money laundering conspiracy, honest services wire fraud, bank fraud, identity theft — diverted ~$9 million from DEA undercover operations to himself and Colombian cartel associates
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Irizarry sentencing
source2_description: Miami Herald coverage of Irizarry case

name: Joseph Bongiovanni
role_title: Special Agent, Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA), Buffalo NY

name: Morteza Amiri

source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Amiri sentencing

name: Gregory McMichael
role_title: Former Investigator (retired), Glynn County Police Department, Georgia
charges: Felony murder (×4), aggravated assault (state); interference with civil rights / hate crime, attempted kidnapping, use of firearm during crime of violence (federal) — participated in killing of Ahmaud Arbery
jurisdiction: Superior Court of Glynn County, Georgia; U.S. District Court, S.D. Georgia
outcome: Life without parole + 20 years state; Life + 7 years federal concurrent
conviction_date: 2022 (state convicted Nov. 2021; sentenced Jan. 7, 2022; federal sentenced Aug. 8, 2022)
source1_description: DOJ Press Release on Arbery case federal sentencing
source2_description: New York Times coverage of Arbery case

name: Javian Griffin
